domain: physics / condensed matter (Anderson localization in disordered lattices)
specific_mechanism: Anderson localization: in a disordered quantum lattice, interference of multiply-scattered waves localizes the eigenstates within a localization length, producing a mobility edge separating extended from localized states; the localization length diverges at the mobility edge as a power law in disorder strength
llm_application: Treat residual-stream activations as wave amplitudes on a token-position lattice with disorder coming from input-token heterogeneity; quantify when an activation feature becomes 'localized' (concentrated on a small token cluster) vs 'extended' (delocalized across the context); use the localization-length statistic on per-feature activations to identify which attention circuits are robust to input perturbation (extended, fragile) vs trap-like (localized, brittle). Diagnostic readout: an Anderson-localization-style mobility edge in feature space.
novelty_claim: Existing residual-stream analysis is mostly normed magnitude or directionality (Anthropic SAE, IOI circuits). No published 2024-2026 work formalizes feature-level activations as Anderson-localized wavefunctions on the token-position lattice with a mobility edge in disorder strength.
